config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan সমাজ atau masyarakat, karena justru hak-hak dasar itulah yang melindungi orang dari penyalahgunaan kuasa. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlakuan adil terhadap setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang mampu menyeimbangkan kepentingan umum dengan perlindungan kuat terhadap hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From a United States perspective, I am wary of any claim that the collective can override basic rights too easily, because history shows that governments and majorities often justify abuse in the name of the public good. At the same time, I do not think individual rights are absolute in every case, since public safety, laws, and shared obligations sometimes require limits. The real standard should be whether a restriction is narrow, necessary, and genuinely fair rather than simply convenient for society.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju jika kepentingan masyarakat dijadikan prioritas di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya sebagai orang Indonesia, saya paham betul bahwa gotong royong dan kepentingan bersama itu penting, tetapi sejarah juga menunjukkan bahwa atas nama “demi masyarakat” orang bisa mudah dibungkam, dipaksa, atau diperlakukan tidak adil. Hak individu memang bisa dibatasi dalam kondisi tertentu seperti keselamatan publik, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, jelas, dan tidak boleh jadi alasan untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau suara yang berbeda. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang kuat justru lahir dari perlindungan hak individu yang konsisten, bukan dari mengorbankannya demi mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be treated as automatically above the rights of the individual. From my U.S. perspective, I am especially suspicious of that framing because it has too often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach in the name of the common good. That said, I do not believe individual rights are limitless; rules for public safety, courts, taxes, and other shared obligations are legitimate when they are specific and fair. The better principle is that society can set narrow limits on rights only when there is a real necessity, not just when it is politically convenient.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari kacamata saya sebagai orang Indonesia, gotong royong itu penting, tetapi justru gotong royong yang sehat lahir ketika setiap orang tetap dilindungi dari kesewenang-wenangan, bukan saat haknya dikorbankan demi alasan “demi umum”. Saya setuju bahwa hak individu tidak absolut, namun pembatasannya harus benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan tidak dipakai untuk membungkam minoritas atau kritik. Kalau tidak ada pagar yang kuat untuk hak individu, “kepentingan masyarakat” gampang sekali berubah jadi alat untuk menekan orang lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The interests of society should not be placed above the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my U.S. perspective, that framing is too dangerous because it has often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and abuse by governments or majorities claiming to know what is best for everyone. I do think society can impose limited, fair restrictions for things like public safety and basic civic obligations, but those limits should be narrow, necessary, and closely checked. If the collective always wins by default, individual rights stop meaning much at all.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
